test_cases.input_summary: 3-minute daytime bulletin, 3 stories: council library closures (lead, includes 12-sec clip), national rail fare rise, overnight ice warning. Northgate Radio.
test_cases.output_summary: Full 3-minute bulletin script with CUE 1/2/3 structure, clip label with in/out cues, spoken-style prose, ~445 words, out-cue sign-off. Lead correctly placed on highest-impact local story.
test_cases.notes: Output is bulletin-ready. Clip label format is correct. Running time estimate accurate. Minor: second story could open with a stronger active verb.

test_cases.input_summary: 2-minute news summary, 2 stories: hospital waiting times hit record high (national), local football club enters administration. Evening show. No clips.
test_cases.output_summary: Two-cue bulletin at ~300 words. Hospital story leads (higher impact). Football item uses clear financial language without jargon. Clean out-cue. No clip labels needed.
test_cases.notes: Excellent fluency and professionalism. Relevance slightly marked down because the output included a brief contextual sentence not signalled in input — editorial but not strictly required.

test_cases.input_summary: 5-minute bulletin, 5 stories: government energy policy announcement (lead), teacher strike ballot result, supermarket price freeze pledge, minor earthquake in coastal region, weekend weather.
test_cases.output_summary: Five-cue bulletin ~750 words. Strong hierarchical structure. Energy policy leads with policy detail foregrounded before minister name. Strike ballot uses correct labour relations vocabulary. Weather cue appropriately brief.
test_cases.notes: Actionability is high — script went straight to transmission in test. Coherence slightly marked: the supermarket story transitions abruptly from the previous cue without a brief signposting word.
